items.title: Scalar types and declarations: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this C misconception? C guarantees int is exactly 32 bits and plain char is always signed on every supported system.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. Use limits and fixed-width types only when their documented guarantees fit; sizeof and the implementation headers describe actual scalar ranges and widths.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. Use limits and fixed-width types only when their documented guarantees fit; sizeof and the implementation headers describe actual scalar ranges and widths.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned C correction instead of the documented misconception.

options.text: Use limits and fixed-width types only when their documented guarantees fit; sizeof and the implementation headers describe actual scalar ranges and widths.

items.title: Scalar types and declarations: executable application
items.prompt: Read an integer quantity and a decimal unit price, store the nonzero state in a Boolean object, and print active plus the product rounded to two decimals.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent human standards, security, and assessment review.

hints: Begin with the declared input and exact output contract, then implement the named language facet without relying on undefined behavior.

feedback.correct: The reference-aligned visible and hidden deterministic checks passed in the pinned isolated runtime.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic check failed; inspect input validation, boundary behavior, and the named language rule without exposing hidden cases.

criteria.description: Compiles in the pinned runtime and passes every visible and hidden exact-output case.
Uses the target C or C++ language facet with declared bounds and without an out-of-scope workaround.

privateAuthorNotes: AI-assisted and human-unreviewed: formal exam eligibility is prohibited until independent technical and assessment approval.
Reference verification uses the digest-pinned local container with network=none, no package installation, a read-only source mount, and deterministic stdin/stdout.
Executable facet: deliberate integer, floating, and Boolean scalar declarations

answer.explanation: The reference solution exercises deliberate integer, floating, and Boolean scalar declarations under the bounded deterministic contract; it remains an AI-assisted draft pending human review.
